gaps.after: a significant impact on small farms within a year.
gaps.explanation: have an impact on 〜 で「〜に影響を与える」。impact と結びつく動詞は have。

gaps.explanation: データが不完全だった→だから実験をやり直した、という結果の流れ。結論を導く therefore が適切。

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Animals That Build for Free
paragraphs: For most of the last century, beavers were treated as a nuisance. Their dams flooded fields and blocked drains, and in many regions they were hunted until almost none remained. Rivers were straightened and lined with concrete so that water would rush away as quickly as possible.

Today that thinking is being quietly reversed. When beavers are allowed to return, the messy ponds and channels they create slow the flow of water, trap sediment, and keep moisture in the land during dry spells. The same dams that once seemed destructive now reduce flooding downstream and create rich habitats where fish, insects, and birds quickly multiply.

Reintroducing them is not without difficulty. A beaver does not read maps, and its dam may appear exactly where a farmer would rather it did not. Yet supporters point out that paying a team of engineers to manage water would cost far more than tolerating a few wet meadows. For a growing number of scientists, the cheapest river engineer is one that works for nothing more than a supply of wood.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Once seen as pests, beavers are now valued for cheaply managing water and habitats.
Beavers should be hunted again because their dams always cause floods.
Concrete channels are the best way to control every river in the world.
Farmers and scientists fully agree that beavers cause no problems at all.

questions.before: During dry periods, the ponds made by beavers help the land keep its

questions.prompt: ビーバーを戻すことの経済的な利点を示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: would cost far more than tolerating a few wet meadows
a beaver does not read maps
rivers were straightened and lined with concrete

questions.sentence: The goal was simple: get the rain to the sea before it could do any damage.

questions.prompt: 筆者のビーバー再導入に対する見方として最も近いものはどれですか。
options: Broadly positive: the benefits are real, even if some practical conflicts remain.
Strongly opposed: beavers bring nothing but expensive destruction.
Completely neutral: the writer takes no side and offers no opinion.
Certain that beavers will soon disappear no matter what people do.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: Tiny Forests in the City
paragraphs: On a small patch of land beside a busy road, a group of neighbours has planted hundreds of young trees, packed tightly together. The plot is no bigger than a tennis court, yet it has been given a grand name: a tiny forest. The idea, first developed in Japan, is now spreading to crowded cities around the world.

These tiny forests are designed to grow fast. Because the trees stand so close, they compete for light and shoot upward more quickly than trees planted far apart. Within a few years, the once-empty corner becomes a cool, green space that softens noise from traffic, offers shade on hot days, and gives birds and insects a place to live.

Residents say the benefits go beyond nature. Schoolchildren help with the planting and return to watch the forest change with the seasons. Neighbours who once barely spoke now meet to pull weeds and water seedlings together. A strip of land that nobody noticed has become a small source of pride for the whole street.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Small, dense forests can quickly improve cities and bring neighbours together.
Cities should remove all trees to make room for wider roads.
Tiny forests take fifty years before they show any benefit at all.
Only farmers in the countryside can plant trees successfully.

questions.before: The trees grow upward quickly because, standing close together, they must

questions.prompt: tiny forest が暑い日にもたらす利点を示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: no bigger than a tennis court

questions.prompt: 第3段落が伝えている主な点はどれですか。
options: Besides helping nature, the project has brought local people closer together.
Schoolchildren are banned from visiting the tiny forest.
The neighbours argue constantly and refuse to work together.
The forest was removed because nobody cared about it.

listeningQuestions.transcript: Would you mind sending me the updated schedule before the end of the day?
The train leaves from platform seven at a quarter past eight.
Had we known about the delay, we would have taken a different route.
The volunteers planted dozens of young trees along the quiet riverbank.
A faint smell of fresh bread drifted through the narrow alley at dawn.
I am not sure whether we should wait for them or start without them.
